title: Ari Emanuel

infobox.value: Ariel Zev Emanuel   (1961-03-29) March 29, 1961 (age 64)[[1]](#cite_note-New_Yorker-1)  [Chicago](/wiki/Chicago "Chicago"), Illinois, U.S.
Sarah Hardwick Addington  ​  ​ (m. 1996; div. 2018)​ [[1]](#cite_note-New_Yorker-1)  [Sarah Staudinger](/wiki/Sarah_Staudinger "Sarah Staudinger")  ​ (m. 2022)​

(born March 29, 1961) is an American businessman and the CEO of [Endeavor](/wiki/Endeavor_(company) "Endeavor (company)"), an entertainment and [media agency](/wiki/Media_agency "Media agency"), as well as CEO and [executive chairman](/wiki/Executive_chairman "Executive chairman") of [TKO Group Holdings](/wiki/TKO_Group_Holdings "TKO Group Holdings"), which owns the [UFC](/wiki/UFC "UFC") and [WWE](/wiki/WWE "WWE").[[2]](#cite_note-2) He was a founding partner of the [Endeavor Talent Agency](/wiki/Endeavor_Talent_Agency "Endeavor Talent Agency") and was instrumental in shaping its June 2009 merger with the [William Morris Agency](/wiki/William_Morris_Agency "William Morris Agency").[[3]](#cite_note-3)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

American businessman and CEO of Endeavor

| Ari Emanuel | |
| --- | --- |
| Emanuel in 2016 | |
| Born | Ariel Zev Emanuel   (1961-03-29) March 29, 1961 (age 64)[[1]](#cite_note-New_Yorker-1)  [Chicago](/wiki/Chicago "Chicago"), Illinois, U.S. |
| Education | [Macalester College](/wiki/Macalester_College "Macalester College") ([BA](/wiki/Bachelor_of_Arts "Bachelor of Arts")) |
| Occupation | Businessman |
| Years active | 2007–present |
| Political party | [Democratic](/wiki/Democratic_Party_(United_States) "Democratic Party (United States)") |
| Spouse(s) | Sarah Hardwick Addington  ​  ​ (m. 1996; div. 2018)​ [[1]](#cite_note-New_Yorker-1)  [Sarah Staudinger](/wiki/Sarah_Staudinger "Sarah Staudinger")  ​ (m. 2022)​ |
| Children | 5 |
| Relatives | [Rahm Emanuel](/wiki/Rahm_Emanuel "Rahm Emanuel") (brother) [Ezekiel Emanuel](/wiki/Ezekiel_Emanuel "Ezekiel Emanuel") (brother) |

**Ariel Zev Emanuel** (born March 29, 1961) is an American businessman and the CEO of [Endeavor](/wiki/Endeavor_(company) "Endeavor (company)"), an entertainment and [media agency](/wiki/Media_agency "Media agency"), as well as CEO and [executive chairman](/wiki/Executive_chairman "Executive chairman") of [TKO Group Holdings](/wiki/TKO_Group_Holdings "TKO Group Holdings"), which owns the [UFC](/wiki/UFC "UFC") and [WWE](/wiki/WWE "WWE").[[2]](#cite_note-2) He was a founding partner of the [Endeavor Talent Agency](/wiki/Endeavor_Talent_Agency "Endeavor Talent Agency") and was instrumental in shaping its June 2009 merger with the [William Morris Agency](/wiki/William_Morris_Agency "William Morris Agency").[[3]](#cite_note-3)

## Early life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ari_Emanuel&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/19/Rahm_and_Ari_Emanuel_at_-fortunetech_%2819487340459%29.jpg/250px-Rahm_and_Ari_Emanuel_at_-fortunetech_%2819487340459%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Rahm_and_Ari_Emanuel_at_-fortunetech_(19487340459).jpg)

Ari (right) participating in a forum alongside his brother Rahm (left)

Emanuel was raised in suburban [Wilmette, Illinois](/wiki/Wilmette,_Illinois "Wilmette, Illinois"). Emanuel is the brother of former [mayor of Chicago](/wiki/Mayor_of_Chicago "Mayor of Chicago"), Presidential Chief of Staff and [U.S. Ambassador to Japan](/wiki/List_of_ambassadors_of_the_United_States_to_Japan "List of ambassadors of the United States to Japan") [Rahm Emanuel](/wiki/Rahm_Emanuel "Rahm Emanuel"), American oncologist and bioethicist [Ezekiel Emanuel](/wiki/Ezekiel_Emanuel "Ezekiel Emanuel"), and sister Shoshana Emanuel (who was adopted). His father, [Jerusalem](/wiki/Jerusalem "Jerusalem")-born Benjamin M. Emanuel, was a pediatrician who was active in the [Irgun](/wiki/Irgun "Irgun") in [Mandatory Palestine](/wiki/Mandatory_Palestine "Mandatory Palestine").[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-5) His mother, Marsha Emanuel (*née* Smulevitz), was a [civil rights activist](/wiki/Civil_rights_activist "Civil rights activist"), and the one-time owner of a Chicago-area [rock and roll](/wiki/Rock_and_roll "Rock and roll") club.[[6]](#cite_note-Bumiller-6) During his third grade at school, Ari was diagnosed with [ADHD](/wiki/ADHD "ADHD") and [dyslexia](/wiki/Dyslexia "Dyslexia").[[7]](#cite_note-7) To supplement instruction at school, his mother spent many hours helping him to learn to read. She hired tutors and private instructors to give him private reading lessons at home.[[6]](#cite_note-Bumiller-6)[[8]](#cite_note-8) Emanuel attended [New Trier High School](/wiki/New_Trier_High_School "New Trier High School") and graduated from [Macalester College](/wiki/Macalester_College "Macalester College") in 1983.[[6]](#cite_note-Bumiller-6)[[1]](#cite_note-New_Yorker-1)

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ari_Emanuel&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Career")]

In his early career, Emanuel worked as an assistant to New York talent agent Robert Lantz and then as a trainee at [Creative Artists Agency](/wiki/Creative_Artists_Agency "Creative Artists Agency") in Hollywood after getting a start working in the company's mail room in 1987.[[9]](#cite_note-9)[[1]](#cite_note-New_Yorker-1) Prior to founding Endeavor, Emanuel was a partner at InterTalent and senior agent at [ICM Partners](/wiki/ICM_Partners "ICM Partners") (ICM). In 1995, Emanuel began a year and a half in recovery after getting hit by a flatbed truck, and was inspired during this period in time to start his own company.[[10]](#cite_note-accidentandmore-10)[[11]](#cite_note-1993accidentandendeavorlaunch-11) In March 1995, Emanuel started Endeavor.[[12]](#cite_note-19935ari-12) Emanuel has served as a member of [Live Nation Entertainment](/wiki/Live_Nation_Entertainment "Live Nation Entertainment") board of directors since September 2007.[[13]](#cite_note-13)

Emanuel has been described as a mogul and power player in Hollywood.[[14]](#cite_note-14)[[15]](#cite_note-15) Emanuel and [Patrick Whitesell](/wiki/Patrick_Whitesell "Patrick Whitesell"), the co-CEO of WME, have both been named to *[Fortune](/wiki/Fortune_(magazine) "Fortune (magazine)")'*s Businessperson of the Year list.[[16]](#cite_note-16)[[17]](#cite_note-17) In a May 2013 article on Emanuel, *Fortune* called him "one of the biggest guns in the consolidating entertainment business".[[18]](#cite_note-18)

Emanuel's relationships with his clients, coupled with his stature in the industry, has led to various homages and parodies over the years, including [Bob Odenkirk](/wiki/Bob_Odenkirk "Bob Odenkirk")'s character Stevie Grant on *[The Larry Sanders Show](/wiki/The_Larry_Sanders_Show "The Larry Sanders Show")*, and [Ari Gold](/wiki/Ari_Gold_(Entourage) "Ari Gold (Entourage)"), played by [Jeremy Piven](/wiki/Jeremy_Piven "Jeremy Piven") on the HBO television show *[Entourage](/wiki/Entourage_(U.S._TV_series) "Entourage (U.S. TV series)")*.[[19]](#cite_note-Horn-19)[[20]](#cite_note-:0-20)

In April 2002 agent Sandra Epstein and other Endeavor employees sued Ari Emanuel on allegations that a pornographic website was operated out of Endeavor's offices and that Emanuel made racist and anti-gay remarks.[[21]](#cite_note-Cieply-21) Emanuel disputed these accusations at the time. Emanuel settled Epstein's claims for $2.25 million.[[21]](#cite_note-Cieply-21)

In 2011, Emanuel co-founded TheAudience with [Sean Parker](/wiki/Sean_Parker "Sean Parker") and [Oliver Luckett](/wiki/Oliver_Luckett "Oliver Luckett").[[22]](#cite_note-Empson-22)

Endeavor went public in 2021, the first Hollywood agency to do so,[[20]](#cite_note-:0-20) with Emanuel owning a stake worth around $480 million according to [*Bloomberg*](/wiki/Bloomberg_L.P. "Bloomberg L.P.").[[23]](#cite_note-23) He and Patrick Whitesell are also co-CEO of [IMG](/wiki/IMG_(company) "IMG (company)"), a global sports, events and talent management company headquartered in New York City.[[24]](#cite_note-24)

In 2023, Emanuel's total compensation from Endeavor was $83.9 million, up 340% from the previous year and representing a CEO-to-median worker [pay ratio](/wiki/Wage_ratio "Wage ratio") of 1,184-to-1 for the company, as well as making Emanuel the seventh highest paid CEO in the US that year.[[25]](#cite_note-25) He debuted on the *[Bloomberg Billionaires Index](/wiki/Bloomberg_Billionaires_Index "Bloomberg Billionaires Index")* in February 2025 with an estimated net worth of over $1 billion.[[26]](#cite_note-26)

In 2025, Emanuel is set to buy [Frieze](/wiki/Frieze_(magazine) "Frieze (magazine)"), a global art fair and publishing group, previously owned by Endeavor. The deal is valued at $200 million and is backed by a group of investors including [Apollo Global Management](/wiki/Apollo_Global_Management "Apollo Global Management") and [RedBird Capital Partners](/wiki/RedBird_Capital_Partners "RedBird Capital Partners").[[27]](#cite_note-27)

## Public advocacy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ari_Emanuel&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Public advocacy")]

Emanuel has hosted fundraisers for the [Democratic Party](/wiki/Democratic_Party_(United_States) "Democratic Party (United States)").[[28]](#cite_note-28) He donated $2,700 to [Hillary Clinton](/wiki/Hillary_Clinton "Hillary Clinton") in the [2016 United States presidential election](/wiki/2016_United_States_presidential_election "2016 United States presidential election"). His long-standing relationship with his former client [Donald Trump](/wiki/Donald_Trump "Donald Trump") is well documented.[[29]](#cite_note-ABCNews-DonaldTrump-Meets-Ari-Emmanuel-29) During the [2016 Republican Party presidential primaries](/wiki/2016_Republican_Party_presidential_primaries "2016 Republican Party presidential primaries"), Emanuel offered to produce a movie for Trump which was considered for the [2016 Republican National Convention](/wiki/2016_Republican_National_Convention "2016 Republican National Convention") but ultimately was not followed through on.[[30]](#cite_note-30)

After the disappearance of *[Washington Post](/wiki/Washington_Post "Washington Post")* journalist [Jamal Khashoggi](/wiki/Jamal_Khashoggi "Jamal Khashoggi") on October 2, 2018, and reports that the [Saudi hit squad](/wiki/Tiger_Squad "Tiger Squad") had [assassinated him inside their consulate in Turkey](/wiki/Assassination_of_Jamal_Khashoggi "Assassination of Jamal Khashoggi"), Emanuel called White House senior adviser and Trump son-in-law [Jared Kushner](/wiki/Jared_Kushner "Jared Kushner").[[31]](#cite_note-31) Emanuel tried to extricate Endeavor from a $400 million deal with the Saudi Arabian government.[[32]](#cite_note-32)

In October 2022, Emanuel urged several businesses to stop working with rapper [Kanye West](/wiki/Kanye_West "Kanye West") over his antisemitic comments.[[33]](#cite_note-33)[[34]](#cite_note-34)

Emanuel voiced critical opinions of Israeli prime minister [Benjamin Netanyahu](/wiki/Benjamin_Netanyahu "Benjamin Netanyahu") when he was awarded the Humanitarian Prize at [Simon Wiesenthal Center](/wiki/Simon_Wiesenthal_Center "Simon Wiesenthal Center")'s National Tribute Gala. Emanuel called for Netanyahu to step down during his speech, which caused a negative reaction from the audience. He also criticized protesters using the slogan "[from the river to the sea](/wiki/From_the_river_to_the_sea "From the river to the sea")" which he claimed was a call for [genocide](/wiki/Genocide "Genocide").[[35]](#cite_note-35)[[36]](#cite_note-36)[[37]](#cite_note-37)

## Philanthropy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ari_Emanuel&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Philanthropy")]

Emanuel has in the past been active on the board of trustees of P.S. Arts, a [Los Angeles, California](/wiki/Los_Angeles,_California "Los Angeles, California")-based [nonprofit organization](/wiki/Nonprofit_organization "Nonprofit organization") that works to bring art education programs to [Southern California](/wiki/Southern_California "Southern California") schools. He has also helped the [Museum of Contemporary Art, Los Angeles](/wiki/Museum_of_Contemporary_Art,_Los_Angeles "Museum of Contemporary Art, Los Angeles"), establish MOCAtv, a dedicated [YouTube](/wiki/YouTube "YouTube") art channel. In 2012, he joined the museum's [board of trustees](/wiki/Board_of_trustees "Board of trustees").[[38]](#cite_note-38)

## Personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ari_Emanuel&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Personal life")]

In 1995 Emanuel was seriously injured after a flatbed truck hit him while he was crossing the street.[[12]](#cite_note-19935ari-12)[[11]](#cite_note-1993accidentandendeavorlaunch-11) The incident resulted in Emanuel flying 20 feet and he was left with multiple broken ribs, a torn ACL and a cracked head. After undergoing a year and a half of recovery he avoided paralysis.[[12]](#cite_note-19935ari-12)[[10]](#cite_note-accidentandmore-10)[[11]](#cite_note-1993accidentandendeavorlaunch-11) His ability to recover from the incident became a source of inspiration for him on how he decided to pursue his career.[[12]](#cite_note-19935ari-12)

In 1996, Emanuel married girlfriend Sarah Hardwick Addington; they have three sons.[[6]](#cite_note-Bumiller-6)[[39]](#cite_note-39) The couple divorced in 2018.[[1]](#cite_note-New_Yorker-1) In May 2022, he married fashion designer [Sarah Staudinger](/wiki/Sarah_Staudinger "Sarah Staudinger"), founder of the Los Angeles label STAUD.[[40]](#cite_note-40) Emanuel adopted the daughter of Shoshana, his adopted sister.[[41]](#cite_note-41)

He follows a strict vegan diet.[[42]](#cite_note-42)[[43]](#cite_note-43)
